name: Achelous

visual_attributes.texture: fluid and smooth when in water form, rough and earthy when in bull form
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: varies with form; large as a bull, expansive as a river
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: fluid and adaptable, changing forms seamlessly

domain_specific_visual.river_association: often depicted with water flowing from his beard
domain_specific_visual.horn_of_plenty: missing a horn when in bull form, symbolizing the cornucopia

functional_attributes.primary_function: god of the largest river in Greece
secondary_functions: father of the Sirens and various nymphs

domain_specific_functional.shape_shifting: can change form to suit different needs or challenges
domain_specific_functional.conflict_with_heracles: engaged in combat with Heracles over Deianeira

relationships.description: Achelous is the son of the Titan Oceanus.
Achelous is the son of the Titaness Tethys.
Achelous is the father of the Sirens.
Achelous is the father of Callirrhoe, wife of Alcmaeon.
Achelous fought Heracles for the hand of Deianeira.

contextual_attributes.origin: Greek mythology, associated with the Achelous River
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Greece, particularly the region around the Achelous River
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: represents the power and fertility of rivers, involved in myths of transformation and abundance

contrastive_constraints: NOT a simple bull without divine attributes
